_doc: License allowlist for the FRONTEND tree, mirroring the backend tomte/configs/tox.ini [Licenses] block (liccheck PARANOID posture). Every installed dependency's license must match `allowedSpdx`, be resolved by `licenseOverrides`, or be listed in `exemptions`. Unknown or unlisted licenses FAIL — they are resolved, not silenced. See frontend/scripts/license-check.mjs and CONTRIBUTING.md ('Supply-chain checks').
_keep_when_empty: Do NOT delete this file when overrides/exemptions are empty. Empty arrays are the running assertion of 'license gate exists AND zero current debt' — pre-paves the schema, preserves git history of past resolutions, keeps the CODEOWNERS-routed surface, and lets cross-repo tooling distinguish 'clean gate' (count=0) from 'not in use' (no file).

_fields.allowedSpdx: SPDX identifiers (plus npm-ecosystem aliases like 'Apache2', 'Apache 2.0') that pass the gate. Mirror tomte [Licenses] authorized_licenses verbatim where it applies to npm. Additions beyond tomte (e.g. OFL-1.1 for @fontsource fonts, Artistic-2.0 for transitive dev tooling) are explicit extensions.
_fields.unauthorizedSpdx: SPDX identifiers that ALWAYS fail, regardless of override or exemption. Mirror tomte unauthorized_licenses verbatim. Default-deny via PARANOID covers anything not on either list; this array is reserved for licenses we want to call out by name.
_fields.licenseOverrides: Package whose declared license is wrong (UNKNOWN, malformed, or the package.json field disagrees with the LICENSE file). Each entry RESOLVES the license to a specific SPDX value — never used to silence a real copyleft. Fields: package (exact name), spdx (resolved SPDX), reason, added (YYYY-MM-DD), review (YYYY-MM-DD).
_fields.exemptions: Packages allowed despite their license being on `unauthorizedSpdx` or otherwise unresolved. Exact NAME match, version-agnostic. NEVER a namespace prefix — every new platform variant of @img/sharp-libvips-* must be added explicitly. Fields: package (exact name), spdx, reason, added (YYYY-MM-DD), review (YYYY-MM-DD).

exemptions.reason: libvips native binary that Next.js next/image loads at request time (dynamically linked, not statically linked into shipped JS). LGPL-3.0 dynamic-linking carve-out applies. CI(linux-x64) platform binary.
Same libvips lib; ARM64 Linux platform binary.
Same libvips lib; Alpine/musl Linux platform binary.
Same libvips lib; Alpine/musl ARM64 platform binary.
Same libvips lib; macOS Intel platform binary.
Same libvips lib; macOS Apple-Silicon platform binary.
Same libvips lib; Windows x64 platform binary.
